en.extract:
The ashy myzomela (Myzomela cineracea) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is found in New Britain and Umboi Island in Papua New Guinea. It was previously considered a subspecies of the ruby-throated myzomela.

The '''ashy myzomela''' ('''''Myzomela cineracea''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]].<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Orenstein |first=Ronald I. |date=1976 |title=Birds of the Plesyumi Area, Central New Britain |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1367698 |journal=The Condor |volume=78 |issue=3 |pages=370–374 |doi=10.2307/1367698 |jstor=1367698 |issn=0010-5422|url-access=subscription }}</ref> It is found in [[New Britain]] and [[Umboi Island]] in [[Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Steadman |first=David W. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=vBZXJQ3HDg0C |title=Extinction and Biogeography of Tropical Pacific Birds |date=2006-10-15 |publisher=University of Chicago Press |isbn=978-0-226-77142-7 |pages=125 |language=en}}</ref> It was previously considered a subspecies of the [[ruby-throated myzomela]].
